fact: July 25 is exactly 206 days into the year, which means there are only 159 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun blazes down for about 15 hours a day in many places!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this time of year in North America, creating a buzzing symphony that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
July 25 falls in the middle of Leo season in the zodiac, when the lion constellation rules the sky.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, this date marks deep winter - penguins are huddling together while kids in the north are swimming!
Sunflowers are usually towering giants right now in northern climates, some reaching 12 feet tall and turning their massive heads to follow the sun like solar-powered robots.
The Earth is actually farther from the sun during Northern Hemisphere summer - we're about 3 million miles more distant than in winter!
Fireflies are typically putting on their best light shows around this date in temperate regions, blinking in perfect patterns to find their summer sweethearts.
In many places, July 25 often brings the hottest overnight temperatures of the year - even at midnight, it might still be 80 degrees outside!
This date sits right in the middle of monsoon season in parts of Asia, when entire cities can get drenched by 10 inches of rain in a single day.
Corn plants are growing so fast around this time in farm regions they make crackling sounds - you can literally hear them stretching taller in the fields!
July 25 is roughly when many northern lakes and rivers reach their warmest temperatures, perfect for swimming but also when algae blooms can turn water bright green.
